visible_agent_inputs.question: United 300 and 300 were both made in what country?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1:
Topic: Farman F.300 and Morane‑Saulnier MS.300 (300-series aircraft)

- Farman F.300: "built in France in the early 1930s."; Morane‑Saulnier MS.300: French trainer, "first flown in 1930."
- Passages contain no mention of "United 300."
- Missing bridge fact: country-of-manufacture relation between the entity "United 300" and the "300" referred to in the question. Possible bridge entities: Farman F.300 (verifies France) and Morane‑Saulnier MS.300 (verifies France); "United 300" is absent/unverified in the passages.
- Next hops (priority): 1) Fetch "United 300" (disambiguation/article) — lede/infobox and any film/parody entries; 2) Fetch "300 (film)" — lede/infobox and "Production"/"Release" sections; 3) If "300" might mean an aircraft, fetch "Farman F.300" and "Morane‑Saulnier MS.300" lede/infobox pages to confirm.

input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

When composing the query, select the minimal anchor token(s) visible in `question` and/or `summary_1` that point to the missing page or fact. You may append a single short attribute keyword (for example country member occupations record season) only when that keyword is needed to disambiguate the evidence type. Do not introduce any other new tokens.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Use these operational rules when choosing a template:
- If summary_1 explicitly lists two or more distinct page anchors as next-hop targets, combine those visible anchor tokens into one compact multi-anchor query and append a short attribute keyword naming the evidence type (for example country member occupations record).
- If summary_1 flags a single missing anchor or a missing_titles list is present, emit a single-anchor metadata-style query that places the missing anchor token first and appends a concise attribute keyword naming the desired fact.
- When summary_1 names multiple candidate entities and the missing fact concerns one candidate’s membership, order, or relation to a canonical collection, prefer a connector-style query that pairs the highest-priority candidate token (first or prioritized in summary_1) with the canonical collection token plus a short attribute keyword requesting membership or order.
- When individual member tokens are present but the needed retrieval is about that individual, prefer member-token + group-anchor + member (or equivalent short attribute) to target the individual’s page rather than issuing a broad group-level query.

Priority rules for ambiguity and disambiguation:
- If summary_1 lists multiple candidate anchors or otherwise signals ambiguity, emit a single connector-style query that concatenates the prioritized anchor tokens (use the order or priority visible in summary_1) and then append exactly one short attribute token or a visible year token for disambiguation.
- If summary_1 contains a comma-separated list of individuals and the missing evidence concerns a member, prefer the earliest-listed individual token paired with the visible group token and append the single attribute token member.
- If the question contains a visible year token that disambiguates a title, include that year token before the attribute token in the query.
- If none of the above applies, emit a single-anchor query using the most relevant visible token and at most one short attribute token when the question implies an attribute.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.

retrieved_doc_snippets: United States military deployments | The military of the United States is deployed in more than 150 countries around the world, with nearly 300,000 of its active-duty personnel serving outside the United States and its territories.

Foxburg Country Club | Foxburg Country Club, established in 1887, is the oldest golf course in continuous use in the United States. It is located in Foxburg, Clarion County, Pennsylvania, United States of America, approximately 55 mi north of Pittsburgh on a hill rising about 300 feet above the Allegheny River. The course was listed in 2007 as Foxburg Country Club and Golf Course on the National Register of Historic Places. The clubhouse contains the American Golf Hall of Fame.
IOMA | IOMA is an evidence-based skincare brand from France. It was founded in 2008 and it is now present in around 300 stores in France and in several other countries like the United States.

Ultimate Waylon Jennings | Ultimate Waylon Jennings is a compilation album by American country music artist Waylon Jennings. It was released by RCA Records on March 23, 2004. The album peaked at number 16 on the "Billboard" Top Country Albums chart. The album has sold 631,300 copies in the United States as of April 2017.
Gale, Indiana | Gale is an unincorporated community in Center Township, Hendricks County, Indiana, United States. Gale is located on the east side of Danville, just along country road 300 east.

Select Registry | Select Registry, Distinguished Inns of North America, is a non-profit association of more than 300 country inns, B&Bs and boutique hotels throughout the United States, Canada and now Europe. Founded in 1972, the association is based in Charleston, South Carolina, United States.

FlipKey | FlipKey is an online vacation rental marketplace. It is a subsidiary of TripAdvisor. In 2016 the company listed more than 300,000 properties in 179 countries. Its headquarters is in Boston, Massachusetts, United States.
source_state.summary_2: The 300 examples in the provided context were made in France. United 300 is not present in the provided passages (its country of manufacture is not given here). Farman F.300 was built in France in the early 1930s.
source_state.answer: The summaries state the "300" examples were made in France; the country of manufacture for "United 300" is not given in the summaries.

output.query: 300 (film) United 300 United 300 and 300 were both made in what country?

retrieved_doc_snippets: United 300 | United 300 is an American short film that parodies "United 93" (2006) and "300" (2007). It won the MTV Movie Spoof Award at the MTV Movie Awards 2007. The short was created by Andy Signore along with some of his friends. During his speech, he said, roughly, "The film was not making fun of a tragedy; it was a tribute to those who stood up against tyranny".

KTM 300 | The KTM 300 is a series of 2-stroke enduro off-road motorcycles made by KTM. They are the Penton-inspired 1984 300 GS, the 300 DMX, the dirt only 300 MXC, European road legal 300 EXC and their current US counterparts the 300 XC (close-ratio) and 300 XC-W (wide-ratio gear box) respectively. The KTM 300 is designed for difficult off-road conditions. The 300 EXC and the 300 XC-W have a small headlight, speedometer, tail-light and somewhat softer linkless PDS suspension. Starting in 2009 a ...[truncated]

Fortschritt ZT 300 | ZT 300 is a series of 20-kN-tractors produced from 1967 to 1984 by the VEB Traktorenwerk Schönebeck. It succeeded the "RS14 Famulus" series and unlike the Famulus, the ZT 300 series was sold under the brand name Fortschritt. "ZT 300" stands for the ZT 300 series tractors as well as the initial tractor Fortschritt ZT 300. In total, 72.382 tractors were made. The most built model was the ZT 303 introduced in 1972 with automatic all-wheel-drive; in the early 1980s it would cost ...[truncated]
British Rail Class 300 | Class 300 was allocated to a proposed fleet of parcels-carrying electric multiple units, which were to be converted from former Class 307 units.

.300 Remington Short Action Ultra Magnum | .300 Remington Short Action Ultra Magnum (also known as 300 RSAUM, 300 RSUM or 300 Rem SAUM) is a .30 caliber short magnum cartridge that is a shortened version of the Remington 300 Ultra Mag, both of which derive from the .404 Jeffery case. The Remington Short Ultra Mag was put on the market shortly after Winchester released its 300 WSM round in 2001, resulting in the Winchester product getting the marketing advantage that has eclipsed the Remington of ...[truncated]

No. 300 Polish Bomber Squadron | No. 300 (Polish) "Land of Masovia" Bomber Squadron (Polish: "300 Dywizjon Bombowy "Ziemi Mazowieckiej"" ) was a Polish World War II bomber unit. It was fighting alongside the Royal Air Force and operated from airfields in the United Kingdom.

300 (film) | 300 is a 2006 American epic war film based on the 1998 comic series "300" by Frank Miller and Lynn Varley. Both are fictionalized retellings of the Battle of Thermopylae within the Persian Wars. The film was directed by Zack Snyder, while Miller served as executive producer and consultant. It was filmed mostly with a super-imposition chroma key technique, to help replicate the imagery of the original comic book.
target_state.summary_2: Both United 300 and 300 were made in the United States. United 300 is an American short film created by Andy Signore. 300 is a 2006 American epic war film directed by Zack Snyder.
